params.name: query
params.description: The (natural language) search query.

params.name: cuisine
params.description: The cuisine(s) of the recipes. One or more, comma separated (will be interpreted as 'OR'). See a full list of supported cuisines.

params.name: excludeCuisine
params.description: The cuisine(s) the recipes must not match. One or more, comma separated (will be interpreted as 'AND'). See a full list of supported cuisines.

params.name: diet
params.description: The diet for which the recipes must be suitable. See a full list of supported diets.

params.name: intolerances
params.description: A comma-separated list of intolerances. All recipes returned must not contain ingredients that are not suitable for people with the intolerances entered. See a full list of supported intolerances.

params.name: equipment
params.description: The equipment required. Multiple values will be interpreted as 'or'. For example, value could be "blender, frying pan, bowl".

params.name: includeIngredients
params.description: A comma-separated list of ingredients that should/must be used in the recipes.

params.name: excludeIngredients
params.description: A comma-separated list of ingredients or ingredient types that the recipes must not contain.

params.name: type
params.description: The type of recipe. See a full list of supported meal types.

params.name: instructionsRequired
params.description: Whether the recipes must have instructions.

params.name: fillIngredients
params.description: Add information about the ingredients and whether they are used or missing in relation to the query.

params.name: addRecipeInformation
params.description: If set to true, you get more information about the recipes returned.

params.name: addRecipeNutrition
params.description: If set to true, you get nutritional information about each recipes returned.

params.name: author
params.description: The username of the recipe author.

params.name: tags
params.description: The tags (can be diets, meal types, cuisines, or intolerances) that the recipe must have.

params.name: recipeBoxId
params.description: The id of the recipe box to which the search should be limited to.

params.name: titleMatch
params.description: Enter text that must be found in the title of the recipes.

params.name: maxReadyTime
params.description: The maximum time in minutes it should take to prepare and cook the recipe.

params.name: minServings
params.description: The minimum amount of servings the recipe is for.

params.name: maxServings
params.description: The maximum amount of servings the recipe is for.

params.name: ignorePantry
params.description: Whether to ignore typical pantry items, such as water, salt, flour, etc.

params.name: sort
params.description: The strategy to sort recipes by. See a full list of supported sorting options.

params.name: sortDirection
params.description: The direction in which to sort. Must be either 'asc' (ascending) or 'desc' (descending).

params.name: minCarbs
params.description: The minimum amount of carbohydrates in grams the recipe must have.

params.name: maxCarbs
params.description: The maximum amount of carbohydrates in grams the recipe can have.

items.docs: Search through hundreds of thousands of recipes using advanced filtering and ranking. NOTE: This method combines searching by query, by ingredients, and by nutrients into one endpoint.

params.name: ingredients
params.description: A comma-separated list of ingredients that the recipes should contain.

params.name: number
params.description: The maximum number of items to return (between 1 and 100). Defaults to 10.

params.name: ranking
params.description: Whether to maximize used ingredients (1) or minimize missing ingredients (2) first.

params.name: ignorePantry
params.description: Whether to ignore typical pantry items, such as water, salt, flour, etc.

items.docs: Ever wondered what recipes you can cook with the ingredients you have in your fridge or pantry? This endpoint lets you find recipes that either maximize the usage of ingredients you have at hand (pre shopping) or minimize the ingredients that you don't currently have (post shopping).

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of results to skip (between 0 and 900).

params.name: number
params.description: The maximum number of items to return (between 1 and 100). Defaults to 10.

params.name: random
params.description: If true, every request will give you a random set of recipes within the requested limits.

items.docs: Find a set of recipes that adhere to the given nutritional limits. You may set limits for macronutrients (calories, protein, fat, and carbohydrate) and/or many micronutrients.

params.name: id
params.description: The id of the recipe.

params.name: includeNutrition
params.description: Include nutrition data in the recipe information. Nutrition data is per serving. If you want the nutrition data for the entire recipe, just multiply by the number of servings.

params.name: addWinePairing
params.description: Add a wine pairing to the recipe.

params.name: addTasteData
params.description: Add taste data to the recipe.

items.docs: Use a recipe id to get full information about a recipe, such as ingredients, nutrition, diet and allergen information, etc.

params.name: ids
params.description: A comma-separated list of recipe ids.

params.name: includeNutrition
params.description: Include nutrition data in the recipe information. Nutrition data is per serving. If you want the nutrition data for the entire recipe, just multiply by the number of servings.

items.docs: Get information about multiple recipes at once. This is equivalent to calling the Get Recipe Information endpoint multiple times, but faster.

params.name: id
params.description: The id of the source recipe for which similar recipes should be found.

params.name: number
params.description: The maximum number of items to return (between 1 and 100). Defaults to 10.

items.docs: Find recipes which are similar to the given one.

params.name: includeNutrition
params.description: Include nutrition data in the recipe information. Nutrition data is per serving. If you want the nutrition data for the entire recipe, just multiply by the number of servings.

params.name: include-tags
params.description: A comma-separated list of tags that the random recipe(s) must adhere to.

params.name: exclude-tags
params.description: A comma-separated list of tags that the random recipe(s) must not adhere to.

params.name: number
params.description: The maximum number of items to return (between 1 and 100). Defaults to 10.

items.docs: Find random (popular) recipes. If you need to filter recipes by diet, nutrition etc. you might want to consider using the complex recipe search endpoint and set the sort request parameter to random.

params.name: query
params.description: The (natural language) search query.

params.name: number
params.description: The maximum number of items to return (between 1 and 100). Defaults to 10.

items.docs: Autocomplete a partial input to suggest possible recipe names.

params.name: id
params.description: The recipe id.

params.name: normalize
params.description: Normalize to the strongest taste.

params.name: id
params.description: The recipe id.

params.name: normalize
params.description: Normalize to the strongest taste.

params.name: rgb
params.description: Red, green, blue values for the chart color.

items.docs: Get an analyzed breakdown of a recipe's instructions. Each step is enriched with the ingredients and equipment required.

params.name: url
params.description: The URL of the recipe page.

params.name: forceExtraction
params.description: If true, the extraction will be triggered whether we already know the recipe or not. Use this only if information is missing as this operation is slower.

params.name: analyze
params.description: If true, the recipe will be analyzed and classified resolving in more data such as cuisines, dish types, and more.

params.name: includeNutrition
params.description: Include nutrition data in the recipe information. Nutrition data is per serving. If you want the nutrition data for the entire recipe, just multiply by the number of servings.

params.name: includeTaste
params.description: Whether taste data should be added to correctly parsed ingredients.

items.docs: This endpoint lets you extract recipe data such as title, ingredients, and instructions from any properly formatted Website.

params.name: includeNutrition

items.docs: This endpoint allows you to send raw recipe information, such as title, servings, and ingredients, to then see what we compute (badges, diets, nutrition, and more). This is useful if you have your own recipe data and want to enrich it with our semantic analysis.

params.name: id
params.description: The recipe id.

items.docs: Automatically generate a short description that summarizes key information about the recipe.

params.name: id
params.description: The recipe id.

params.name: mask
params.description: The mask to put over the recipe image ("ellipseMask", "diamondMask", "starMask", "heartMask", "potMask", "fishMask").

params.name: backgroundImage
params.description: The background image ("none","background1", or "background2").

params.name: backgroundColor
params.description: The background color for the recipe card as a hex-string.

params.name: fontColor
params.description: The font color for the recipe card as a hex-string.

items.docs: Generate a recipe card for a recipe.
Generate a recipe card for a recipe.

This endpoint allows you to break down instructions into atomic steps. Furthermore, each step will contain the ingredients and equipment required. Additionally, all ingredients and equipment from the recipe's instructions will be extracted independently of the step they're used in.

params.name: language
params.description: The language of the input. Either 'en' or 'de'.

items.docs: Classify the recipe's cuisine.

params.name: q
params.value: salmon with fusilli and no nuts
params.description: The recipe search query.

items.docs: Parse a recipe search query to find out its intention.

params.name: ingredientName
params.description: The ingredient which you want to convert.

params.name: sourceAmount
params.description: The amount from which you want to convert, e.g. the 2.5 in "2.5 cups of flour to grams".

params.name: sourceUnit
params.description: The unit from which you want to convert, e.g. the grams in "2.5 cups of flour to grams". You can also use "piece", e.g. "3.4 oz tomatoes to piece"

params.name: targetUnit
params.description: The unit to which you want to convert, e.g. the grams in "2.5 cups of flour to grams". You can also use "piece", e.g. "3.4 oz tomatoes to piece"

items.docs: Convert amounts like "2 cups of flour to grams".

params.name: language
params.description: The language of the input. Either 'en' or 'de'.

items.docs: Extract an ingredient from plain text.

params.name: id
params.description: The recipe id.

params.name: defaultCss
params.description: Whether the default CSS should be added to the response.

items.docs: Visualize a recipe's nutritional information as HTML including CSS.

params.name: title
params.description: The title of the dish.

items.docs: Estimate the macronutrients of a dish based on its title.

params.name: language
params.description: The language of the input. Either 'en' or 'de'.

items.docs: Retrieve the glycemic index for a list of ingredients and compute the individual and total glycemic load.

params.name: q
params.value: How much vitamin c is in 2 apples?
params.description: The nutrition related question.

Take any text and find all mentions of food contained within it. This task is also called Named Entity Recognition (NER). In this case, the entities are foods. Either dishes, such as pizza or cheeseburger, or ingredients, such as cucumber or almonds.

params.name: query
params.description: The query to search for. You can also use partial queries such as "spagh" to already find spaghetti recipes, articles, grocery products, and other content.

items.docs: Search spoonacular's site content. You'll be able to find everything that you could also find using the search suggestions on spoonacular.com. This is a suggest API so you can send partial strings as queries.

params.description: The search query.

items.docs: Search all food content with one call. That includes recipes, grocery products, menu items, simple foods (ingredients), and food videos.

params.description: The maximum price for the specific wine recommendation in USD.

params.name: maxPrice
params.description: The maximum price for the specific wine recommendation in USD.

params.description: The minimum rating of the recommended wine between 0 and 1. For example, 0.8 equals 4 out of 5 stars.

params.description: The number of wine recommendations expected (between 1 and 100).

items.docs: Get a specific wine recommendation (concrete product) for a given wine type, e.g. "merlot".
